sector_specific_levers.lever_name: Clinical Workforce Mix Optimisation (FTE vs. Agency Nursing & Locum Doctors)
condition_precedents: Clinical staffing ratio analysis completed (FTE vs. agency ratio by department and shift)
Agency nursing cost benchmarked vs. full-time equivalent cost including benefits
HR and clinical leadership sign-off on optimised staffing model (patient safety maintained)
industry_sources: McKinsey & Company — The Future of Healthcare Workforce (mckinsey.com/industries/healthcare) — clinical staffing mix optimisation 8-18%
MoHFW — National Health Workforce Account 2023-24 (mohfw.gov.in/) — nurse-to-doctor ratio and staffing benchmarks India
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Model clinical staffing ratios (nurse:bed, doctor:OP visit) by specialty vs NABH and peer hospital benchmarks; identify over/under-staffed departments
Redesign rosters to align staffing with patient volume patterns (peak vs off-peak hours, seasonality); reduce agency/locum dependency
Implement workforce management software for demand-driven scheduling; integrate with HIS for real-time census data
Track agency locum spend, overtime %, and nurse turnover monthly; set targets for reduction

diagnostic_signals.signal: Agency/locum spend >15% of total clinical payroll indicating chronic staffing-model dependency on high-cost contractors
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Permanent staffing model and roster redesign will reduce cost per clinical FTE

diagnostic_signals.signal: Nurse-to-bed ratio >20% above NABH requirement in general wards during off-peak hours
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Demand-linked rostering will reduce excess clinical labour cost

diagnostic_signals.signal: Clinical overtime as % of base payroll >12% for ≥3 consecutive quarters
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Rostering and shift-pattern optimisation will reduce overtime premium

required_data_fields: Clinical FTE roster by specialty, shift, and employment type (permanent vs agency/locum)
Patient census data (ADC, OP visits) by department and time of day
Agency/locum spend by vendor and specialty (12 months)
NABH staffing norm requirements by bed type
Nurse turnover and tenure data

sector_specific_levers.lever_name: Medical Supplies & Implant Cost Optimisation (GPO vs. Direct Sourcing)
condition_precedents: Medical supplies spend classified by category (consumables, implants, reagents, pharmaceuticals)
GPO membership evaluated vs. direct contract savings for top 10 categories by spend
Clinician preference item (CPI) committee established — physician engagement on implant standardisation
industry_sources: NABH — Accreditation Standards for Hospitals 5th Edition (nabh.co/) — supply chain and materials management standards
McKinsey & Company — Procurement in Healthcare (mckinsey.com/industries/healthcare) — GPO vs. direct sourcing savings 10-20%
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Audit formulary: map all consumables (sutures, gloves, IV fluids, implants) to category, vendor, unit price, and annual volume
Benchmark unit prices against NHA/CGHS rates and comparable hospital group purchases; identify price gaps by item
Consolidate vendors for top-20 spend categories; negotiate group purchasing agreements or join existing hospital GPO
Mandate formulary compliance via HIS-linked requisition; track savings by category and vendor compliance monthly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Medical consumable cost as % of revenue >18% vs peer benchmark of <15% for similar case mix
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Procurement rationalisation will yield material cost reduction

diagnostic_signals.signal: Same consumable item purchased from >3 vendors at different unit prices (price spread >20%)
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Consolidation will harmonise pricing and improve negotiating leverage

diagnostic_signals.signal: Implant cost >40% of procedure revenue for high-volume specialties (orthopaedics, cardiology)
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Implant vendor consolidation and consignment model will reduce working capital and purchase cost

required_data_fields: Formulary with item description, vendor, unit price, and annual consumption volume
NHA/CGHS benchmark prices for common consumables and implants
Spend by vendor and category (12 months)
HIS requisition and inventory management configuration
Existing GPO or group purchasing membership details

sector_specific_levers.lever_name: Pharmacy Cost Optimisation (Formulary Management & Generic Substitution)
condition_precedents: Formulary committee established with clinical pharmacy leadership
Generic substitution rate benchmarked vs. hospital peer group (target > 70%)
Prescriber behaviour analytics deployed — high branded prescribers identified and coached
industry_sources: NPPA — National List of Essential Medicines (NLEM) 2022 (pharmaceuticals.gov.in) — drug price control and generic substitution framework
MoHFW — Jan Aushadhi Scheme Data 2024 (mohfw.gov.in/) — generic drug penetration benchmarks
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Audit pharmacy formulary for therapeutic substitution opportunities: identify branded drugs with generic equivalents at >50% cost savings
Evaluate empanelment with 340B/NHA tender pricing programs; renegotiate pricing with top-10 pharma vendors by spend
Implement pharmacist-led formulary management: enforce generic substitution protocols with prescriber education
Track pharmacy cost per admission, generic substitution rate, and drug wastage monthly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Pharmacy cost per admission >20% above peer benchmark for similar case mix index
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Formulary management and generic substitution will reduce cost

diagnostic_signals.signal: Generic substitution rate <60% where bio-equivalent generics are available and clinically appropriate
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Systematic generic substitution will reduce drug cost without clinical compromise

diagnostic_signals.signal: Drug wastage and expiry write-off >2% of pharmacy procurement value annually
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Formulary optimisation and demand-linked ordering will reduce waste

required_data_fields: Formulary with drug name, branded vs generic, unit cost, and annual consumption
Generic availability and bioequivalence data by therapeutic category
Pharmacy vendor contracts and pricing
Prescribing pattern data by physician and specialty
Drug expiry and wastage report (12 months)

sector_specific_levers.lever_name: Revenue Cycle Management Automation (Denial Management & Pre-Auth)
condition_precedents: Denial rate benchmarked by payer and denial reason (target < 5% first-pass denial)
Pre-authorisation workflow mapped and automated triggers configured
Revenue cycle platform selected (Athena, Epic Resolute, or equivalent)
industry_sources: NABH — Clinical Establishment Standards 2023 (nabh.co/) — revenue cycle and billing standards
Ardent Partners — AP Metrics That Matter 2024 (ardentpartners.com/ap-metrics-that-matter-in-2025/) — process automation benchmarks applicable to revenue cycle
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Measure denial rate by payer, denial reason, and department; quantify revenue at risk from denials and late submissions
Implement RCM automation: eligibility verification, pre-authorisation, and coding audit tools integrated with HIS/billing system
Automate claim submission and denial workflow with real-time payer response tracking; resolve top-3 denial reasons with system controls
Track denial rate, days in AR, net collection rate, and bad debt by payer monthly; set improvement targets

diagnostic_signals.signal: Initial claim denial rate >10% of submitted claims, indicating billing or coding quality gap
diagnostic_signals.confirms: RCM automation and coding improvement will recover revenue

diagnostic_signals.signal: Days in accounts receivable >45 days for insurance payers vs benchmark <30 days
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Process automation will accelerate cash conversion

diagnostic_signals.signal: Revenue cycle team >15 FTEs with >60% of time on manual claim follow-up and denial management
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Automation will release FTE capacity and reduce cost per claim processed

required_data_fields: Claim denial data by payer, denial reason, and department (12 months)
Accounts receivable aging by payer and bill type
Revenue cycle FTE count and time allocation by activity
HIS/billing system architecture and integration capability
Payer contract terms and adjudication rules by payer

sector_specific_levers.lever_name: Clinical Space Utilisation (OT, ICU & Bed Occupancy Optimisation)
condition_precedents: OT utilisation data available (target > 75% first-case on-time starts)
Bed occupancy tracking system deployed (ADT system with real-time census)
Theatre scheduling optimisation model implemented — case sequencing and block booking
industry_sources: MoHFW — Health Facility Statistics 2023-24 (mohfw.gov.in/) — bed occupancy rates and OT utilisation benchmarks India
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Measure OT and procedure-room utilisation by specialty: scheduled hours, actual cases, and turnaround time (TAT) between cases
Redesign OT scheduling: implement block scheduling with performance guarantees, reduce TAT through standardised turnover protocol
Reconfigure underutilised clinical space: convert low-utilisation OTs to day-surgery or procedure rooms; expand high-demand specialty
Track OT utilisation rate, cases per OT per day, and revenue per OT hour monthly

diagnostic_signals.signal: OT utilisation rate <70% of available hours with TAT between cases >30 minutes
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Scheduling improvement and TAT reduction will increase cases per day without capital investment

diagnostic_signals.signal: Revenue per OT hour below peer benchmark for comparable case mix
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Optimised scheduling will improve OT revenue intensity

diagnostic_signals.signal: Underutilised clinical space (empty beds, unused OTs) contributing fixed cost without revenue
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Reconfiguration will improve return on clinical assets

required_data_fields: OT scheduling data: planned vs actual cases, start times, TAT, and cancellations
Clinical space inventory with utilisation rates by room type
Revenue per procedure by OT room and specialty
Staffing model for OT and procedure rooms
Bed occupancy rate by ward and specialty

sector_specific_levers.lever_name: Medical Equipment Asset Optimisation (Lease vs. Buy & Maintenance Cost)
condition_precedents: Medical equipment asset register updated with utilisation data (target > 65% utilisation)
Lease vs. buy TCO analysis completed for high-value equipment (CT, MRI, linear accelerators)
Preventive maintenance programme optimised — OEM vs. independent service provider benchmarked
industry_sources: NABH — Medical Equipment Management Standards (nabh.co/) — medical equipment lifecycle and maintenance benchmarks
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Build equipment utilisation register: map every capital medical device to scheduled hours, actual usage, and age; flag equipment <40% utilised
Evaluate pooling model for under-utilised equipment across departments or campuses; assess feasibility of shared scheduling
Retire or dispose of equipment below utilisation threshold; negotiate performance-based service contracts (up-time SLA) replacing time-and-material AMCs
Track equipment utilisation, AMC cost per equipment, and unplanned downtime quarterly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Capital medical equipment utilisation <50% for ≥30% of the installed base
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Pooling or disposal will reduce ownership cost

diagnostic_signals.signal: AMC spend as % of equipment value >8% for equipment >7 years old
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Switching to performance-based or capex-refreshed model will reduce maintenance cost

diagnostic_signals.signal: Equipment downtime events >5/quarter causing case cancellations or delays
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Performance-based SLA contract will improve uptime and reduce revenue loss

required_data_fields: Equipment inventory with age, capital value, utilisation rate, and AMC cost
Equipment downtime and maintenance log (12 months)
AMC contract terms by vendor and equipment type
Sharing feasibility assessment (location proximity, scheduling compatibility)
NABH and clinical requirement for dedicated vs shared equipment

source_verification_note: industry_sources entries indicate the published works that directionally support each lever's savings thesis. Where no external benchmark was found, INTERNAL_CALIBRATION is noted. All cited sources are real publications; specific savings percentages remain internal calibrations unless the source directly cites a matching range.
